subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Antibiotic Resistance
description: A micro-lesson that makes Antibiotic Resistance usable.

hook.text: By 2050, antibiotic-resistant bacteria could kill 10 million people per year—more than cancer.

buildup.text: Antibiotics kill susceptible bacteria but leave resistant ones alive to multiply unopposed.

discovery.text: Bacteria share resistance genes with each other—even across different species—through plasmid transfer.

twist.text: No new antibiotic class has been discovered since 1987. We're running out of weapons.

climax.text: Finishing prescribed courses and never using antibiotics for viral infections slows resistance.

punchline.text: Overuse creates superbugs. Underinvestment leaves us unarmed. Barely solvable.

quiz.question: How do bacteria develop antibiotic resistance?
options: Surviving bacteria multiply and share resistance genes with others
